scope: src/agents_sync/mcp_secret_policy.py + tests/test_mcp_real_adapters.py (+ call-site spot-check in sync.py, mcp_server_io/{render,parse,headers,_helpers}.py)
verdict_rationale: Zero CRITICAL findings, zero un-mitigated MAJOR findings; all six prior MAJOR items (CQ-01, CQ-02, CQ-03, CQ-04, CQ-07, CQ-12, CQ-13) are closed or downgraded to WARNING/INFO with explicit, documented residual scope.
remediation_diff_summary: The v0.5 remediation widened the safe env-name character class to mixed case, replaced fixed-depth header detection with a parent/leaf rule that works at any nesting depth, added NFKC+casefold key normalization, broadened SECRET_FIELD_RE (passphrase, credentials, dsn, cookie, jwt, pat, private_key, etc.), and added a value-side HIGH_CONFIDENCE_SECRET_VALUE_RE check that fires both on ordinary keys and inside the env_var/env_http_headers allowlist via _is_safe_env_var_name_value. List indices are now first-class ints in the path tuple with bracket rendering in field_path. apply_mcp_secret_policy now deep-copies on every return path and accepts an injectable warning_cache. The real-adapters test gained a hard 'literal-token not in persisted_text' assertion across claude/codex/opencode/state/canonical artifacts plus an exact secret_redactions payload check.
detailed_analysis: The remediation closes the structural and security-heuristic gaps that drove the prior AUDIT_WEAK verdict. CQ-01 (UPPER_SNAKE-only env names) is closed by widening _ENV_NAME to '[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*'. CQ-02 (anchored safe-reference regex rejecting concatenated env refs) is closed by is_safe_secret_reference. CQ-03 (over-permissive env_var/env_vars/env_http_headers allowlist) is closed by _expects_env_var_name + _is_safe_env_var_name_value. CQ-04 closed on three axes — SECRET_FIELD_RE expansion, _normalize_key NFKC+casefold, and value-side HIGH_CONFIDENCE_SECRET_VALUE_RE. CQ-05 (dead client_secret branch): closed by deletion. CQ-06 (fragile depth detection): closed by parent/leaf rule. CQ-09 (return-value ownership): closed via deep-copy on every path. CQ-10 (format_env_reference validation): closed. CQ-11 (list-index path encoded as bare str): closed. CQ-12 (test pins placeholder but not absence): closed. CQ-15 (round-trip pin): closed. CQ-07 (module-level cache) partially mitigated — back-compat shim with documented DI path; WARNING. CQ-13 acceptable as suite-level regression guard now exists. Persisting findings: CQ-08 (redact placeholder still has original_env_var=None, no forensic mapping back from placeholder to source field at canonical-state read time) and CQ-14 (no end-to-end test through Syncer.sync_once() with literal and 'refuse' policy asserting McpSecretLeakError). Both WARNING. CQ-17 (magic two-letter dir codes in fixture) remains INFO. No new findings emerged in the call-site spot-check.

findings.description: Redact mode still emits redactions with original_env_var=None unconditionally. Placeholder env vars are per-call positional ('AGENTS_SYNC_REDACTED_1', '_2', ...) with no preserved mapping back to which canonical field or original-env name the placeholder represents.
findings.recommendation: When the original value was already an env reference, preserve env_reference_name(value) into original_env_var. When the original was a literal, record at minimum the field_path role and a content fingerprint (sha256 prefix) so the same literal across multiple poll cycles gets the same placeholder.

findings.category: I2 Dead Code / missing test coverage of documented behavior
findings.description: _config() sets mcp_server_secret_policy='refuse' by default, but no test in this file feeds a literal-secret-bearing input through Syncer.sync_once() under that policy and asserts that McpSecretLeakError surfaces.
findings.recommendation: Add one test that writes the same {'env': {'GITHUB_TOKEN': 'literal-token'}} fixture under the default 'refuse' policy and asserts McpSecretLeakError is raised by sync_once() AND that no codex/opencode artifact was written.

findings.description: _PERMISSIVE_WARNING_CACHE is still a module-level mutable set. The remediation added an injectable warning_cache parameter, but Syncer.sync_once still uses the module-level cache and clears it via reset_mcp_secret_warning_cache() on every poll. Two Syncer instances in the same process still share warning-deduplication state.
findings.recommendation: Give Syncer its own per-instance set and pass it explicitly through to render.py / parse.py call sites; once no production call site uses the module-level fallback, delete _PERMISSIVE_WARNING_CACHE and reset_mcp_secret_warning_cache().

findings.category: M2 Long fixture / I4 magic strings
findings.description: The 13 two-letter directory codes ('ca','cc','cs','cr','xa','xp','xs','xr','as','oa','oc','os','or') are magic strings whose meaning is implicit in the surrounding config-key mapping.
findings.recommendation: Replace the inline tuple with a dict {'claude_agents_dir': 'ca', ...} so the directory code is colocated with the config key that consumes it.
